tagline: A static site built by your own build.sh — any toolchain, hosted by Snapshot.

pages.title: Built by build.sh
pages.lede: This whole site was generated by a plain Node script in GitHub Actions — no Julia, no Pluto, no framework. Snapshot ran build.sh and published the dist/ folder it produced.

cards.p: Node, Python, Hugo, Zola, a Makefile, a bash heredoc — if it writes HTML to dist/, Snapshot hosts it.
The build executes in your own GitHub Actions. Snapshot never runs your build code on its servers — it only ingests the output.
snapshot.toml with type = "build" (or just drop a build.sh at the repo root). That's the whole contract.
type = "build" tells Snapshot this repo is a custom build. [app] output picks the folder to publish (default dist/).
Snapshot runs build.sh at the repo root. Here it just calls `node build.mjs`, which reads content.json and writes styled HTML into dist/.
Snapshot zips dist/, attests it, and serves it on the sandbox bundle origin — fast on the edge, with the platform's size + safety limits applied to the output.
